title: Support chat invite delivery fix
description:
When support staff clicked Start chat, the admin side showed "Chat invite sent" but the customer never got the accept popup. The invite was created in the database but the customer browser did not run the incoming-invite path — especially for admin-capable accounts and when the session tenant did not match the customer's presence tenant.

This update fixes invite delivery: the API returns pending invites to the customer participant first, sessions are stored under the correct tenant from the presence list, and all users poll for incoming chat invites (not only non-admin accounts).

Who is affected: PipeShare support chat on the private OVH server (pipeshare.live). SaaS tenants receive this when the next platform version is applied in cPanel releases.
changeLog: Customer browsers poll for pending support chat invites regardless of admin role.
Chat/active API prioritizes pending sessions where the user is the invited customer.
Chat start uses the customer's presence tenant so invites reach the right SSE channel.
Chat respond finds sessions by participant, not only strict tenant middleware scope.
Admin support UI no longer shows "invite sent" when the user is the invitee.
